beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: It clicked that a lone eigenstate is silent because the magnitude of e^(-iEt/hbar) is always 1, and only superposing two energies makes the cloud slosh at the Bohr frequency. I want to reproduce your box numbers. Can you (1) derive the 0.376 eV and 1.505 eV levels for a 1 nm infinite square well, (2) show the cross term in the probability density that oscillates at (E2 - E1)/hbar, and (3) compute the emitted wavelength and confirm it lands in the deep ultraviolet?

props.command: It clicked that a lone eigenstate is silent because the magnitude of e^(-iEt/hbar) is always 1, and only superposing two energies makes the cloud slosh at the Bohr frequency. I want to reproduce your box numbers. Can you (1) derive the 0.376 eV and 1.505 eV levels for a 1 nm infinite square well, (2) show the cross term in the probability density that oscillates at (E2 - E1)/hbar, and (3) compute the emitted wavelength and confirm it lands in the deep ultraviolet?

beats.narration_text: A quantum state called 'stationary' — nothing moves. Or so it seems.

card.headline: Why a Stationary State Isn't Standing Still

beats.narration_text: One energy eigenstate: its probability cloud is frozen. Completely flat. Nothing changes. The phasor spins — but you cannot see it.
beats.note: Single phasor arrow rotating in complex plane, |ψ|² bar flat on right panel

beats.narration_text: But atoms emit light at sharp frequencies. If stationary states don't oscillate, where do the spectral lines come from?

card.headline:
If a stationary state never oscillates,
where do spectral lines come from?

beats.narration_text: Classically, a vibrating charge radiates at its oscillation frequency. A static charge radiates nothing. A frozen electron should be silent.
beats.note: Classical: oscillating charge emits (arrow), static dot emits nothing (X)

beats.narration_text: One eigenstate: |ψ|² is flat in time. The phase rotates at E/ℏ — but |e^{-iEt/ℏ}| = 1. The rotation is invisible. There is nothing to see.
beats.note: Single phasor at E1 rotating; probability density panel stays flat/constant

card.headline: Two phases beat at the Bohr frequency

beats.narration_text: Add state 1 and state 2. Each phase rotates at its own rate. Their difference rotates at (E₂ − E₁)/ℏ — the Bohr frequency. The cross term in |ψ|² oscillates at exactly that rate.
beats.note: Two phasors E1 and E2 rotating; difference vector oscillates; |ψ|² panel shows sloshing

beats.narration_text: The probability cloud rocks back and forth. The electron's center of charge oscillates. An oscillating charge radiates — at frequency (E₂ − E₁)/h. That is the spectral line.
beats.note: Probability density rocking left-right; emission arrow out; frequency label

beats.narration_text: Every spectral line, the same story.

card.headline: Every spectral line is a beating pair of phases

beats.narration_text: The hydrogen Balmer series — four visible lines — each one a different pair of energy levels. Red: n=3 to n=2. Blue-green: n=4 to n=2. The phases beat, the electron oscillates, the photon escapes.

props.headline: The hydrogen Balmer series — four visible lines — each one…

beats.note: STILL·ai: hydrogen spectrum showing four Balmer lines colored and labeled with n transitions

beats.narration_text: A single eigenstate is silent because its phase is unobservable alone. Only when two eigenstates coexist does the relative phase become real — and it ticks at the Bohr frequency.
beats.note: Left: one phasor — silent. Right: two phasors — beating, ticking clock symbol

beats.narration_text: E₁ and E₂ for a 1 nm box: 0.376 eV and 1.505 eV. Difference: 1.129 eV. Frequency: 2.73 × 10¹⁴ Hz — deep ultraviolet. The probability cloud sloshes back and forth 273 trillion times per second.
beats.note: Box with energy levels; E1 and E2 labeled; ΔE = 1.13 eV; f = 2.73e14 Hz labeled

beats.narration_text: A stationary state is not static. Its phase rotates invisibly. Superpose two and the rotation becomes real — a beating, oscillating probability cloud. That beat is every spectral line ever measured. That is quantum mechanics.

card.headline:
The phase rotates. Two phases beat.
Every spectral line is that beat.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. E₁ and E₂ for a 1 nm box: 0.376 eV and 1.505 eV. Difference: 1.129 eV. Frequency: 2.73 × 10¹⁴ Hz — deep ultraviolet. The probability cloud sloshes back and forth 273 trillion times per second. A stationary state is not static. Its phase rotates invisibly. Superpose two and the rotation becomes real — a beating, oscillating probability cloud. That beat is every spectral line ever measured. That is quantum mechanics.

artifactLines: E₁ and E₂ for a 1 nm box: 0.376 eV and 1.505 eV.
A stationary state is not static.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [the mechanism just described] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
